transcript: My name is Kash, I am the podcast host over here at Jupiter, and today I'm going to be talking about growing onchain finance. Hopefully, my 54 slides will work. There we go. All right, here's the truth. Nobody ships like Jupiter, which is great news for our users, it is great news for the ecosystem, it is terrible news for me personally right now. Because in the next 10 minutes, I have to go through five different launches along with two announcements, a million dollar campaign, and a special surprise at the end. So I am going to talk pretty fast, please try to bear with me. Let's start with something we can all agree on: the shift to onchain finance has already begun. You're going to hear more about this over the next three days, it is going mainstream right now, and another fun fact, no one is better positioned for this change than Solana itself. Solana uniquely has the appetite, the users, the builders, the capital allocators. It has the infrastructure that's actually needed for scale at the consumer level and composability across. And critically, it has a world-class suite of products that can help make it actually work. But, with historical shifts become entirely new problems. And that's what we're really focused on over at Jupiter. We are right now the leading on— we are building the future of onchain finance across all of these different verticals, and it's going fairly well so far. We are the leading onchain financial platform. We've done more than a trillion dollars in volume this year alone. We're number one by TVL on Solana according to DefiLlama, and we've had nearly 35 million wallets actually connect to Jupiter services so far. The reason for these numbers: we are the only team that has a full stack of applications, 12 world-class products across a full suite of surfaces: web, mobile, and API. And as nice as these numbers might look on this slide here, the truth is that we are still tiny. In order for onchain finance to actually go mainstream, we need to 10 to 100x from here. There's a lot that still needs to be done. And that's why every day at Jupiter, we ask ourselves, what does onchain finance actually need to continue to grow? And that's what I'm going to go through for the rest of this talk. So let's jump into it. The first thing. What we know is that asset issuers need a trusted data layer for token information. 30,000 new tokens are created every day on Solana on average. It is a dark forest, as many of you actually know right now. And if you are trying to navigate that complexity as an actual good faith actor, whether you're launching a memecoin, an RWA, a commodity, or a project token, it can be very difficult. So today, we are launching, I'm very excited to say, Jupiter VRFD, Jupiter Verified, live into production. Jupiter Verified— yeah, thanks. Jupiter Verified exists to solve this problem of information across. It is built on the shoulders of our ecosystem strict list, which has powered Solana for the last three or four years, keeping millions of traders safe. That's the first part of Verified over here as well. It's going to continue. We have a full-time killer team that's evaluating all of this in real-time to keep traders safe. But that's not all. We also are now adding for the first time ever metadata updates for your token. As your asset continues to evolve, your community continues to evolve, it's important that your holders have access to the best possible information, which is what they can get with Verified. And, one last piece of this here, we are also launching Verified Insights, so that you can highlight the canonical information that your holders need to know about what is going on with your project and with your asset over time. This is all live right now at verified.jup.ag. And the best part is, it's also available via our API, the Pro API in particular, which is going to enable any builder who's out there to bring high-quality data and content into their project as well. All right, that's the first thing. Next up, onchain finance needs more world-class yield products. About four months ago, we launched something called Jupiter Lend. Does anyone here use Jupiter Lend? Can I see a hand? Yeah, all right, my guy. That's good to hear. It has been a raucous time. Yield is one of the main exports of onchain finance as a whole. And Jupiter Lend, we believe, is simpler for lenders and better for borrowers, and it looks like the market agrees. In just four months, we reached $1.6 billion in total market supply. And the number I'm really proud of: 99,000 total positions created. A bunch of net new people coming on who've never used borrow/lend protocols before. And over this time, we've gotten tons of feedback from the community, we've been shipping like crazy, doing all these security audits, and that is why today, I am incredibly excited to announce that Jupiter Lend is officially coming out of beta. Thank you. The product has gotten better. In just four months, we've done six different audits with four independent auditors, and we have two formal certifications on the way as well. So we feel very confident in the security and the stability of the platform, which is why we can also say that as of today, I think it just went live two minutes ago, Jupiter Lend is now open source as well. The code is available, jup.ag/lend. Yeah, let's give it up for open source. It's good stuff, right. This is just the beginning. We're out of public beta, that means that the shipping speed is only going to increase from here, not slow down. Please come to my talk tomorrow at the Money Summit where we have a bunch more announcements that I could not fit into this 10-minute talk without Ellie and the gang murdering me. So, in addition to yield, we also need onchain finance to continue to have more onchain trading activity. I love ETFs and ETPs as much as the next guy, but if the end state of this industry is everyone trading ETFs on centralized exchanges, we have not fulfilled on our mission. So we want to bring more people onchain with better trading experiences. That is why today, we are launching Jupiter Terminal. This is an evolution on top of the Jupiter Pro tool that many people have used in the past. We're trying to build a Bloomberg-like experience for getting onchain. This is the single best onchain trading experience you are going to find anywhere. As of today, we are launching my favorite feature: Wallet Tracking. Highly requested by the community. Real-time streaming updates for any wallet on Solana on what they are buying and selling. You can get it right there and notified as it actually happens. In addition, using our world-class data infrastructure and our data indexing power—and shout out to the team on this—AlphaScan has gotten even better with more customizations across the data itself and the displays. Plus, Terminal also makes things easier for folks. We know that not everyone is a degen deep in the trenches, so there is now a toggle mode on where you can actually go between Trench Mode and Classic Mode to make trading even easier. Again, this is live right now at jup.ag/terminal. And if that's not enough to get you excited, we've got a nice little icing on this cake as well. Which is, today we are also launching the Jupiter Rewards Hub. For the first time ever, you are going to be able to get rewarded for using Jupiter and referring your friends to use it as well. Trading, referrals, rewards, all put together in a single place to reward people for what they're doing and as they bring more people onchain. And to kick it off, to show how much we believe that onchain trading really matters, we are putting up, as Jupiter, a $1,000,000 prize pool for the Rewards Hub. Anyone who trades on Terminal, who trades on Mobile, or the Wallet will be eligible to get a piece of this prize pool. I'll share how this works very quickly. Basically, the more that you trade or the more that you refer, the more of these cards that you earn. Each card has a randomized value from $1 to $10,000, which means you don't have to trade millions and millions of dollars in order to be able to earn some really great prizes. Go check this out, the competition ends January 31st, 2026. jup.ag/rewards. If you want to join the program with your own token, if you have an asset as well, please reach out to JoeDoe on X and we can help, you know, co-incentivize and get more people trading on here. In the short term, this is a Jupiter product. In the longer term, we want to open this up so that anyone in the ecosystem who has a token who wants to use Jupiter's distribution will be able to tap into it, and so we can help grow the pie altogether. That's coming up as well. Speaking of some builders though, we really do believe that building is going to be the future of Solana, right? It is builders who build apps and those apps is what brings the users. I'm old enough to remember when there were only 72 developers over on Solana. As I look around this crowd right now, it seems like a little bit more than 72 folks. And that is why we want to continuously improve the API experience for builders. Jupiter's APIs are the most used, the most integrated across all of DeFi. Our partners have made more than $750 million using our APIs. And today, that's getting another major upgrade. We are officially launching the Jupiter Developer Platform. For the builders in particular, we'll like this one I think. Yeah. The Jupiter Developer Platform gives you all the information you need to know about the APIs and gives them superpowers. On top of the APIs themselves, which are world-class, you are also now going to have comprehensive real-time analytics on every swap, every API request, and so on that happens. This gives you complete visibility into what's actually going on with your application. And better than that, you're going to have detailed logs and debugging so that you can fix problems before they start. Building onchain—if you are building onchain, you really should be building with Jupiter. It is going to be the best place to do it. You can go check this out live right now, portal.jup.ag. This just launched. Yeah, shout out to Anmol in the crowd here as well. All right, a few more to go through. Another thing that onchain finance needs to grow, we believe, is stablecoins with deep product integrations. Over the next two or three days, you're going to hear a lot of talks about stablecoins. Stablecoins are great, but you need to have a virtuous flywheel around the usage in order to make them sticky and adopted by large swaths of people. And so we believe that stablecoins with deep product integrations are going to be the ones that actually win in the long run. A little while ago, we announced our own stablecoin, JupUSD, built for onchain finance, built in partnership with the friends over at Ethena. Now, JupUSD is not a yield-bearing stablecoin itself, but what we're really excited about doing is finding ways to share some of those underlying economics from the Jupiter product suite back to people who hold it. So, if you can use it in Lend, Mobile, and Wallet, you'll be able to share some of that economic upside. And integrations into the Jupiter ecosystem allow us to do really cool things like earning rewards while you wait for your prediction market to actually settle if you're using JupUSD for it. And today, I'm excited to announce JupUSD will be launching late next week with live activities in trading and earning, and then a third use case is going to go live in Q1 of next year. This one is going to be really fun. Yeah, JupUSD. All right, the timer is already red, so I'm going to go to my one more thing. As we are trying to grow onchain finance, we need a team that is able to do that. We need more high-quality builders who understand Solana and being onchain very deeply. And that is why I am thrilled to announce that Jupiter has acquired Rain.fi as of just now. Yeah, shout out to Rain.fi. The best, a lot of fans in here, that's great to see. For those who don't know, Rain.fi is one of the most innovative DeFi protocols in Solana. They've been building for more than four years, 230,000 loans activated on their peer-to-peer lending marketplace. And together with them, we are going to be building towards a future where there is a money market for every asset. Borrow/lend protocols like Lend, like Jupiter Lend, have certain risk requirements. You need deep liquidity, it has to be on there for a little while, and so on. But we believe in a future where every asset onchain is productive. That's how you make things 10x better than traditional systems, where every memecoin, every RWA, and every commodity can actually earn yield as well. And so together, we are going to be building Jupiter Offerbook, which is a peer-to-peer lending offering where you make loans directly to others, you set your own APYs around any collateral that exists. More details are going to come on this very soon. It's going to be launching in Q1 of next year, but we're incredibly excited about what it's going to add to the Solana DeFi ecosystem. So, in short, here's how we are advancing onchain finance right now: high-quality data with Jupiter Verified, live today; world-class yield, Jupiter Lend out of beta; trading experiences like Bloomberg with Jupiter Terminal; builders like the Stripe for crypto with the Jupiter Developer Platform; a DeFi native stablecoin with JupUSD; and making every asset onchain productive with Jupiter Offerbook. This is all stuff that's coming right now with us. I will say, if you liked this 10 minutes and you want to see two days of it, please join us at Catlumpurr, our community conference in Kuala Lumpur at the end of January. It's going to be an absolute blast. You'll get tons of announcements, insights, community events. It's going to be super fun. And honestly, this is just the start. We are going to keep building until the entire world is saying the same three words when they want to get onchain: Just Use Jupiter. Thank you very much, everybody.

enrichment.generated_query: Jupiter Verified Jupiter Solana Breakpoint 2025 (blog OR official announcement OR documentation OR docs)

tags.impact_reason: Jupiter's launch of VRFD aims to solve a critical problem in the Solana token ecosystem, and given Jupiter's existing footprint, this is a major update.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the planet Jupiter as the semantic anchor because it directly references the company's name and serves as a powerful metaphor for its role as a central aggregator with a growing ecosystem of products ('moons') like Rain.fi and JupUSD. The story is about building a 'full-stack' infrastructure, and a planet represents a foundational, gravitational center. I included the diegetic text '$1T VOL' projected holographically onto the planet's surface to highlight the massive scale mentioned in the transcript ('trillion dollars in volume') and integrate it into the futuristic, data-driven world of the artwork.
visual_idea.description: A monumental, technologically-advanced planet Jupiter dominates the 3:4 frame, viewed from a low angle. Its surface is not gas, but a vast expanse of brushed titanium panels etched with a glowing, intricate network of data-traces reminiscent of a server motherboard. Encircling the planet are vast, shimmering rings composed of holographic financial tickers and data points. Two distinct moons orbit in the mid-ground, one glowing with a cyan hue (Rain.fi) and another with a stable, golden light (JupUSD). Integrated diegetically onto the planet's immense surface, a massive holographic projection reads '$1T VOL', appearing as a live system status update. The background is the deep, dark void of space, with distant, faint nebulae providing a sense of infinite depth.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, technologically-advanced planet Jupiter dominates the 3:4 frame, viewed from a low angle, emphasizing its colossal scale and height. Its surface is not gas, but a vast expanse of brushed titanium panels, meticulously etched with a glowing, intricate network of data-traces reminiscent of a server motherboard. Encircling the planet are vast, shimmering rings composed of dynamic holographic financial tickers and complex data points, streaming and shifting with vibrant light. Two distinct moons orbit in the mid-ground; one glows with an ethereal cyan hue, bearing deeply integrated, luminous characters forming 'Rain.fi', while the other radiates a stable, golden light, with its surface displaying the precisely etched, glowing characters 'JupUSD'. Integrated diegetically onto the planet's immense surface, a massive holographic projection of '$1T VOL' appears as a live system status update, its characters formed by intricate light patterns floating in the atmosphere. The background is the deep, dark void of space, with distant, faint nebulae providing a sense of infinite depth. The scene is illuminated with dramatic, cinematic volumetric lighting, creating high-contrast chiaroscuro effects and emphasizing realistic materials and photoreal detail. The overall atmosphere is moody and epic, resembling a celestial orrery, rendered with hyperrealistic precision. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the planet Jupiter as the semantic anchor because it directly references the company's name and serves as a powerful metaphor for its role as a central aggregator with a growing ecosystem of products ('moons') like Rain.fi and JupUSD. The story is about building a 'full-stack' infrastructure, and a planet represents a foundational, gravitational center. I included the diegetic text '$1T VOL' projected holographically onto the planet's surface to highlight the massive scale mentioned in the transcript ('trillion dollars in volume') and integrate it into the futuristic, data-driven world of the artwork.
visual_idea.description: A macro 1:1 shot focusing on the intersection of the techno-planet's surface and its holographic ring. The foreground details the texture of the brushed titanium, with glowing circuitry pulsing within its etched lines. A dense stream of shimmering, abstract data-points from the ring cuts diagonally across the frame, casting a soft, ethereal light and complex refractions onto the metallic surface below. The depth of field is extremely shallow, blurring the planet's immense curvature into a dark, abstract background.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A perfectly symmetrical, centrally focused, tight macro 1:1 shot. The frame is completely filled with the intricate intersection of a techno-planet's surface and its holographic ring. In the extreme foreground, the meticulously detailed texture of brushed titanium dominates, featuring glowing circuitry pulsing with vibrant energy within its precisely etched lines. A dense, shimmering, abstract stream of holographic data-points cascades diagonally across the entire frame from the ethereal ring, acting as a dramatic, cinematic volumetric light source. This data stream casts a soft, ethereal, yet intense light and complex refractions onto the metallic surface below, creating high-contrast chiaroscuro patterns and glowing light rays that pierce the scene. The light emphasizes the realistic materials and micro-details of the titanium. The depth of field is extremely shallow, blurring the planet's immense curvature into an abstract, dark, and enigmatic background, hinting at a vast, celestial orrery-like structure. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, cinematic volumetric lighting, volumetric fog, ethereal glow.

editorial.headline: Jupiter Acquires Rain.fi, Unveils JupUSD and Institutional Data Suite
editorial.standfirst: The Solana aggregator pivots to full-stack financial infrastructure, acquiring a lending protocol and launching a native stablecoin to capture the entire on-chain lifecycle.
key_takeaways: Jupiter acquires **Rain.fi** to launch "Offerbook," a peer-to-peer lending market for long-tail assets arriving in Q1 2026.
**JupUSD**, a stablecoin built with **Ethena Labs**, launches next week with deep integrations across Lend and Mobile.
**Jupiter Terminal** and **Jupiter Verified** go live immediately, offering Bloomberg-style trading tools and a trusted data layer.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Jupiter is no longer just a swap aggregator; it is effectively becoming the commercial bank and Bloomberg terminal of the Solana network. In a keynote packed with product launches, the clear signal to institutional capital is vertical integration. By launching a verified data layer (**Jupiter Verified**) and a professional trading interface (**Jupiter Terminal**), the protocol is systematically removing the friction and opacity that keeps large allocators off-chain. The introduction of real-time wallet tracking and verified token metadata directly addresses the "dark forest" risks that plague institutional entry.

The acquisition of **Rain.fi** marks a significant expansion in Jupiter's credit strategy. While the newly open-sourced **Jupiter Lend** handles highly liquid "blue chip" assets (already commanding $1.6B in supply), the integration of Rain.fi’s technology into the upcoming "Offerbook" targets the long tail of the market. This peer-to-peer model aims to unlock capital efficiency for the thousands of memecoins, RWAs, and commodities that traditional pool-based lending cannot service due to liquidity risks. This allows Jupiter to service the entire asset spectrum, from USDC to the most volatile speculative tokens.

Simultaneously, the introduction of **JupUSD**, in partnership with **Ethena Labs**, creates a native economic engine for the ecosystem. Unlike a generic stablecoin, JupUSD is designed to be deeply embedded in Jupiter’s margin and lending products, creating a sticky liquidity flywheel. By controlling the issuance, the trading venue, and the lending market, Jupiter is positioning itself to capture value at every layer of the user's financial journey, effectively building a closed-loop economy on top of Solana.
